flashcards.back: The analysis of a single variable at a time to understand its distribution, central tendency, and dispersion.
A measure of how spread out the data is from the mean, calculated as the square root of the variance.

flashcards.front: What does a box plot show?
flashcards.back: The five-number summary: minimum, first quartile (Q1), median, third quartile (Q3), and maximum values.

flashcards.front: When should you use median instead of mean?
flashcards.back: When the data has outliers or is skewed, as the median is less affected by extreme values.
The range between the first and third quartiles (Q3 - Q1), representing the middle 50% of the data.
